headline: Tech Rotation Swings Back Toward Software
summary: A trade that has punished software stocks in favor of semiconductors is reversing this week.  An iShares ETF tracking software stocks is up roughly 7% for the week, while another ETF tracking semiconductor producers has dropped about 5.7%—a more than 12-percentage-point gap on pace for the largest weekly software outperformance on record.  Software's beaten-down names such as ServiceNow, Intuit and Salesforce—which are each down more than 15% so far this year—were among the S&P 500's best-performing stocks Thursday.

headline: ServiceNow AI Push Spans Enterprise Telecom And Healthcare As Valuation Lags
summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) has launched its Autonomous Workforce AI suite and EmployeeWorks, aimed at coordinating AI specialists to handle complex enterprise workflows end to end. The company is expanding its AI driven telecom operations through a partnership with NTT DOCOMO and StarHub to support real time autonomous resolution of roaming issues for travelers. ServiceNow is also partnering with Autonomize AI to develop AI powered healthcare workflow solutions. ServiceNow enters this product...
